{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanmaki.nara.jp/soshiki/shakai_kyoiku/gyomu/rekishi_bunkazai/560.html Kanmak town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00125.html Nara Prefectural History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanmaki.nara.jp/soshiki/shakai_kyoiku/gyomu/rekishi_bunkazai/560.html Kanmak town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00125.html Nara Prefectural History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja}}

The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanmaki.nara.jp/soshiki/shakai_kyoiku/gyomu/rekishi_bunkazai/560.html Kanmak town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00125.html Nara Prefectural History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja}}

Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanmaki.nara.jp/soshiki/shakai_kyoiku/gyomu/rekishi_bunkazai/560.html Kanmak town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00125.html Nara Prefectural History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja}}

The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji ware]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanmaki.nara.jp/soshiki/shakai_kyoiku/gyomu/rekishi_bunkazai/560.html Kanmak town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00125.html Nara Prefectural History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Yamato Kofun Group and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanmaki.nara.jp/soshiki/shakai_kyoiku/gyomu/rekishi_bunkazai/560.html Kanmak town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00125.html Nara Prefectural History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kanmaki, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kanmaki.nara.jp/soshiki/shakai_kyoiku/gyomu/rekishi_bunkazai/560.html Kanmak town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00125.html Nara Prefectural History and Cultural database]{{in lang|ja}}

Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[Kashiba Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Wakayama Line]].

The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[:en:Kashiba Station|Kashiba Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[:en:Kashiba Station|Kashiba Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Wakayama Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[:en:Kashiba Station|Kashiba Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Cluster]][[en:Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Cluster]][[ja:上牧久渡古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65247993]] Wikidata name: Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65247993]] Wikidata name: Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name =上牧久渡古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg; File:Kanmaki Kudo Kofun Group, enkei.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = 上牧久渡古墳群 | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|34.55169444444444|N|135.70936111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Kanmaki|qid=Q1203559}}| region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|06|N|135|42|34|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster'''|上牧久渡古墳群|}} is a group of eight [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kanmaki neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]], [[Nara prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2015.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/267442|title=上牧久渡古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kanmaki Kudo Kofun cluster is located on an independent hill about 70 meters above sea level at the southwestern end of the Umami Hills in the western [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It consists of at least eight tombs, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (Kofun No. 1), six circular Kofun (Kofun No. 2, 4–8), and one square Kofun (Kofun No. 3).
Of the group of tumuli, Kofun No. 1 is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Details are unclear because the tumulus was significantly damaged in modern times, but it is estimated to have been built around the early Kofun period. The mound has been confirmed to have a long axis of approximately 33 meters and a short axis of approximately 28 meters. Based on the topography, it is possible that it was originally approximately 60 meters long.
The square tumulus, a {{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (Kofun No. 3), is located at the tip of the ridge on the northern side of the hill and is thought to have a mound measuring about 15 meters on each side. Three composite wooden coffins were confirmed in the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and from the composite wooden coffin located in the center of the mound, a Chinese pictorial band divine beast [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], as well as fragments of an iron spear, iron sword, iron arrowhead, and a [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery jar. Based on these artifacts, it is believed to have been built in the mid-3rd century. The mirror is the same as the one excavated at the east chamber of the [[Izumi Koganezuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is also considered to be one of the oldest ''kofun'' in the central-western Nara Basin, and is notable for its different size, structure, and location from the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster and Yanagimoto Kofun Group in the plains of the southeastern Nara Basin, which was the center of the Yamato kingship in the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]] at the time. The excavated items were designated a Nara Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property in 2016.
The other tumuli in the cluster are {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular mounds dating from the late 6th century to the mid-7th century.
The site is about 1.5 kilometers north of [[:en:Kashiba Station|Kashiba Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
